title: TikTok Legal Communications (White House Office of the National Cyber Director)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: TikTok Legal Communications (White House Office of the National Cyber Director)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

• All communications sent to and received from the email address: legal@tiktok.com. Please include any preservation requests, subpoenas, court orders, and search warrants, as well as any referrals or notifications provided by TikTok.

• All communications received from any representative or employee of TikTok

• All notifications provided by any representative or employee of TikTok, including any Incident Response Specialists.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Craig Bonder

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Craig Bonder

Best regards,
ONCD FOIA

title: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Data Practices Compliance Official
Minnesota Department of Health
St. Paul, MN

Dear Data Practices Compliance Official,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Minnesota Department of Health's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by MDH employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing MDH employee use of Microsoft Copilot Chat or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Minnesota Department of Health oversees public health policy, disease surveillance, health regulation, and licensing. AI tools may be used for policy analysis, regulatory drafting, epidemiological research, and public communications. MNIT confirmed that Copilot usage data is held by each agency individually, and we are directing this request to MDH for its own employees' usage.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). MNIT has confirmed that Copilot usage data for individual agencies is held by each agency, not centrally by MNIT. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

This is to acknowledge receipt of your data request.  It has been assigned ID#26-101. Your request has been forwarded to the relevant divisions or staff to determine whether the Minnesota Department of Health (“MDH”) has data responsive to your request.  We will be in touch once we know more to discuss next steps.

Future communications or inquiries about your request should be directed to Health.DataPracticesRequest@state.mn.us<mailto:Health.DataPracticesRequest@state.mn.us>.

communications.subject: MDH Data Practices Request ID#26-101 Response
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

This is in response to your data practices request, assigned request ID#26-101. Please find attached data responsive to item 2 of your request. MDH does not have Microsoft CoPilot activated and does not have licenses for other generative AI chatbot tools, thus there is no data responsive to items 1 or 3 of your request.

Your request is now closed. Thank you!
MDH Data Practices Team

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All Statements of Financial Interests filed with the Office of State Ethics for filing years 2024 and 2025, submitted through the date this request is fulfilled. This includes filings by all filers designated by the Governor and under Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-83.

Based on the filing indexes shared at https://www.oseapps.ct.gov/sfi/,  I've attached a spreadsheet identifying the individual filings by name, agency, filing year, filing date, and filing type, in case that may be helpful in processing this request.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: FW: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Nielsen,

This is to acknowledge your Freedom of Information request of February 16, 2026, to the Office of State Ethics (OSE) for copies of all Statements of Financial Interests (SFIs) for a list of 3,175 filer names for calendar years 2024 and 2025.  SFIs are filed annually and are due May 1 for the preceding calendar year.  Therefore, the calendar year 2025 filings will be due May 1, 2026, and we cannot fulfill this portion of your request.  However, we do have all filings for calendar year 2024.  Because your listing contains some duplicate names (persons who may hold a state of Connecticut position and also serve on a board) there are approximately 2,916 individuals currently listed in our database.  This may vary slightly from the number of required filer names as of May 1, 2025, but OSE will provide all existing records responsive to your request.

Although we can attach PDF copies of selected SFIs in satisfaction of smaller Freedom of Information requests using email, the state of Connecticut Information Technology protocol prohibits uploading or interacting with non-state of Connecticut systems.  For larger requests such as yours, the only method we have to complete the request is to save the PDF copies to one or more computer disks.  These can be mailed to a physical address, if you can provide one such that the disks will reach you.  The only cost for this is the replacement cost for the blank media we use, which is $1 per disk.  An invoice will be included with the mailing.  I anticipate that the disk(s) can be prepared no later than February 25, 2026 and mailed out to you on that date, if you can respond to this email with a physical address.

Sincerely,


___________________________
Diane P. Buxo
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division, Office of State Ethics
165 Capitol Avenue
Hartford, CT 06106
Tel: (860) 263-2416 Ofc

Ethics Matters!<http://www.ct.gov/ethics>
[cid:image001.jpg@01DC9FEE.B9233F40]<https://portal.ct.gov/>
LEGAL NOTICE:  Certain communications or records received by or sent from this electronic mail account may be subject to public disclosure pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-200 et seq.  If you have received this in error, please notify us immediately by return email and promptly delete this message and its attachments from your computer system.

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Buxo, 

Thank you so much for your help. Given the constraints for transferring a larger dataset, I'm happy to receive the documents on discs. If it may even be easier, for a larger capacity, I'd be happy to purchase a sealed USB. 

Can you send the disc(s) (or whatever best serves) to: 
Audrey Nielsen 
76 Brush Mill Trl 
Higganum, CT 06441 

Your explanation on the total count makes perfect sense. RE: 2025 filings, it was my understanding from the data summary on the website that ~123 filings for 2025 had been filed ahead of the deadline. It's totally possible I misunderstood the dashboard, though. Would it be possible to arrange a similar production for all 2025 filings filed by deadline in advance, for once they're producible after May 1, 2026? I know this is not an obligation under the FOI act, but in case it might be benefit for allocating time and resources. And of course I would appreciate it. 

I appreciate your time and help. 

Best, 

Audrey

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Nielsen,

Thank you for providing me with a physical address, and I will get the disk or disks out to you for calendar year 2024 filings as soon as possible.

As to the calendar year 2025 filings, some of which may be filed prior to May 1, it would be best (in terms of time expended, since you are requesting all the filings – rather than just those for one agency, for example) that I follow up to have our IT Department prepare a disk or disks for you shortly after May 1, 2026 so that you will get most (if not all) of the filed SFIs.  You are correctly that generally, because OSE is a smaller agency, we ask requestors to follow up with a new FOI request after the annual filing date has passed.  However, in this case I will keep your request for the entire calendar year 2025 filing in a pending file to be worked on shortly after May 1.  There are always a few individuals who didn’t receive a reminder email at their employing agency and who file a bit after the deadline.  There are often a few others who are considered actually delinquent, rather than just late filers, but because there are close to 3,000 separate filings, IT downloads the SFIs in bulk and if there is a particular name of interest to you who isn’t included, you can query about it after you’ve received the disk and looked over its data.

Since I asked you for a physical address, I should note that our office is now located at 165 Capitol Avenue, Suite 1200, Hartford, CT 06106. Our emails and telephone numbers did not change, and my direct extension is noted below.

Sincerely,



___________________________
Diane P. Buxo
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division, Office of State Ethics
165 Capitol Avenue
Hartford, CT 06106
Tel: (860) 263-2416 Ofc

Ethics Matters!<http://www.ct.gov/ethics>
[cid:image001.jpg@01DCA016.F4145490]<https://portal.ct.gov/>
LEGAL NOTICE:  Certain communications or records received by or sent from this electronic mail account may be subject to public disclosure pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-200 et seq.  If you have received this in error, please notify us immediately by return email and promptly delete this message and its attachments from your computer system.

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Thanks so much for your help with this request! I really appreciate it. I wanted to confirm that I received the filings CD in the mail, was able to access the files, and will send the disc back in the envelope provided. 

I really appreciate your help with the upcoming filings, and the timeline constraints you mentioned make sense to me. 

Sincerely,
Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Nielsen,

Thanks so much for confirming that you received the disk.  You don’t need to return it (but can do so if you wish); the replacement cost is $1.00.  OSE does not usually reuse disks, but if you return it I can ask IT to erase those files, if you’ve saved them already, and then I can follow up to use it for the 2025 SFI files when they are available.

Sincerely,


___________________________
Diane P. Buxo

Best, 

Audrey

communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your request to review County records.  On May 29, 2026, we received your Open Records Act request for the following documents:

All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Axon and Fusus that were active any time between 1/1/2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

After reviewing your request, it has been determined that requested documents maintained by Purchasing and Contracting are subject to release under the Open Records Act, except as noted below and is attached.

The County and its officials and departments do not produce documents or records that are protected by the attorney-client privilege or the attorney work product doctrine under Georgia or federal law or that are otherwise exempt from production under the Georgia Open Records Act.  The County expressly reserves and does not waive any statutory exemptions from disclosure provided by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72, et seq. and other applicable laws.


Thank You,

[Home | DeKalb County GA]

title: Records on Facilitation of Return Police 2018 to Present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records on Facilitation of Return Police 2018 to Present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Recently the agency appears to have made extensive changes to the "Return Policy" first announced in Supreme Court's decision in Nken v. Holder. For example, its "Frequently Asked Questions" for this topic (https://www.ice.gov/faq/
facilitating-return) was taken offline in March, 2017 and has since been returned with numerous edits and changes. One of these answers appears to shift responsibility for facilitation of return from the Enforcement and Removal Operations
generally to its "Custody Management Division" specifically. We are concerned with these changes, which have neither been formally announced nor subjected to notice and comment. They appear to indicate both a reduced willingness to
comply with the Solicitor General's reassurances following the Nken decision on the facilitation of return and to indicate that ICE intends to place returned individuals into ICE custody.

Accordingly, I request the following under the Freedom of Information Act:

1. Statistics on facilitation of return requests (also called FoRR Inquiries) from 1/1/09 to present including the following:

a. Number of requests received for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
b. Number of requests granted for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
c. Number of requests denied for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

communications.subject: ICE FOIA Request 2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
July 14, 2020 Matthew Hoppock MuckRock News MuckRock News DEPT MR 98146 411A Highland Ave Somerville, MA 02144 RE:     ICE FOIA Case Number 2020-ICFO-63645  Dear Mr. Hoppock: This acknowledges receipt of your July 13, 2020, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to changes to the agency's "Return Policy" (see request for details). Your request was received in this office on July 13, 2020. Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10- day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you care to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 
Your request has been assigned reference number 2020-ICFO-63645. Please refer to this identifier in any future correspondence. To check the status of an ICE FOIA/PA request, please visit . Please note that to check the status of a request, you must enter the 2020-ICFO-63645 tracking number. If you need any further assistance or would like to discuss any aspect of your request, please contact the FOIA office. You may send an e-mail to ice-foia@ice.dhs.gov, call toll free (866) 633-1182, or you may contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Fernando Pineiro, in the same manner. Additionally, you have a right to right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974. You may contact OGIS as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. (http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status) http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status
Regards, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009 
Telephone: 1-866-633-1182 
Visit our FOIA website at www.ice.gov/foia

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
12/18/2024 

Matthew Hoppock 
MuckRock News DEPT MR 98146 
Somerville, Massachusetts 02144 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2020-ICFO-63645 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 7/13/2020, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to changes to the agency's "Return Policy" as well as 1. Statistics on facilitation of return requests (also called FoRR Inquiries) from 1/1/09 to present including the following: 

a. Number of requests received for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017. 
b. Number of requests granted for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017. 
c. Number of requests denied for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.  (see request for additional details).  Your request was received in this office on 7/13/2020. 

By letter, dated 7/14/2020, we advised you that due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we could encounter some delay in processing your request. We sincerely apologize for the delay and any inconvenience it may have caused. 

Before we begin the time-consuming review process, we want to ensure that you are still interested in continuing the processing of this request. Please indicate your continued interest in pursuing these records within 30 days from the date of this letter, or we will assume you are no longer interested in this FOIA request, and the case will be administratively closed. 

Your request has been assigned reference number 2020-ICFO-63645. Please refer to this number any future correspondence. If you have any questions please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at (866) 633-1182 or 500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009, Washington, D.C. 20536-5009. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
Yes, even though it's been over 4 years, we are still requesting these documents. They should be produced immediately, as the statutory deadline has long since passed. Thank you. 

Matthew Hoppock

Matthew Hoppock

RE: ICE FOIA 2020-ICFO-63645

Dear Requester:

This email is in response to your status inquiry regarding ICE FOIA 2020-ICFO-63645, in which you have requested records pertaining to changes to the agency's "Return Policy" as well as 1. Statistics on facilitation of return requests (also called FoRR Inquiries) from 1/1/09 to present including the following:

a. Number of requests received for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
b. Number of requests granted for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
c. Number of requests denied for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017. (see request for additional details).

As of today, your request has been assigned to a processor and your request is being reviewed. If responsive records have been located, they will be reviewed for a determination of releasability.

ICE FOIA apologizes for the delay in the processing of your request. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

If you have any questions, please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at the address above or (866) 633-1182. Additionally, you have a right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974. You may contact OGIS as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448.

Your request has been assigned reference number 2020-ICFO-63645. Please use this number in future correspondence.

Sincerely,

ICE FOIA Office
Immigration and Customs Enforcement
Freedom of Information Act Office
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: CTS - Delaware
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of each CTS Individual Report that your forensics laboratory received from Collaborative Testing Services (CTS) for CTS Test No. 23-5262 (Firearms Examination). CTS is a company that creates and distributes proficiency tests for forensic laboratories. It sends standardized test samples to labs and then provides reports showing how each examiner performed. The "Individual Report" is the completed assessment, which includes the examiner's answers.
I am requesting the individual report for each examiner who took the test at your agency. For instance, if several examiners took CTS Test No. 23-5262, I would like a copy of each examiner's report.
Additionally, I am requesting this report for every forensic lab in your agency. If your agency has multiple locations or satellite labs, I need the test results for examiners at those sites too.
CTS provides this report directly to the laboratory through the CTS portal and by email when the test cycle closes.
To aid you in your search, I recommend contacting your laboratory’s designated Lab Account Owner (Primary Contact) with CTS. CTS assigns each lab a Primary Contact when the lab account is created, and this person is the individual to whom CTS ships test materials and sends all reports. The Primary Contact has default access to the CTS portal features that contain the Individual Reports. The Primary Contact also receives an emailed pdf copy of the individual report.
Please provide the report in electronic format. Please acknowledge receipt of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Sachi McClendon.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DoD Congressional Correspondence re: USS Liberty
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I was aboard the USS Liberty when the IDF attacked our ship on June 8, 1967 and serve my shipmates as Historian of the USS Liberty Veterans Association.

In the attached extract from the “Department of Defense Office of the Secretary of Defense Congressional Correspondence 2008 to 2011” there is a reference highlighted by a red rectangle.

We request all the correspondence and documents related to that reference.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #25-F-3178
communications.communication:
In the referenced document there is a line that says:

“SUBJECT: CONCERNS REGARDING USS LIBERTY VETERANS FILED A FORMAL REPORT WITH DOD”

The formal report referenced is “Report:War Crimes Committed Against U.S. Military Personnel, June 8, 1967” we filed with the Department of Defense on June 8, 1967. [See https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/22135684-wcr-with-exhibits/]

Since the War Crimes Report was filed in on June 8, 2005, the beginning date for your search should be 6/8/2005.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the following public records, which were recently produced under a previous public records request APD2025-441. 

-Policy 430 ALPR.pdf
-APD_Axon Fleet Contract.pdf

In the previous request APD2025-441, the City of Ashland was asked for:
 (1) all policies CITY/COUNTY NAME has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares (2) a copy of any and all invoices CITY/COUNTY NAME has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares (3) a map of all ALPR cameras that CITY/COUNTY NAME owns, operates or has access to (4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares 

It is my understanding that the City does not possess any additional records related to the above request, and therefore, the only responsive records were Policy 430 ALPR.pdf and APD_Axon Fleet Contract.pdf. However, if you have additional records request to (1), (2), (3), or (4), please produce those as well.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

title: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Data Practices Responsible Authority
City of Minneapolis
350 S. Fifth Street, Room 301
Minneapolis, MN 55415

Dear Data Practices Responsible Authority,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the City of Minneapolis's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Microsoft Copilot.

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, Minn. Stat. § 13.03, we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City of Minneapolis employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing City of Minneapolis employee use of Microsoft Copilot or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees, including the Minneapolis Police Department.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has deployed AI assistants to more than 22,000 state employees and approved Microsoft Copilot Chat as a tool for handling non-public data within the Microsoft Government Community Cloud (GCC) environment (see MNIT, "2025 Annual Report," https://mn.gov/mnit/about-mnit/legislative-reports/2025-annual-report.jsp; see also MnDOT, "Generative Artificial Intelligence Standards," https://www.dot.state.mn.us/policy/it-data/it-003.html). The City of Minneapolis, including the Minneapolis Police Department, has reportedly generated over 37,000 items using Copilot. Minnesota police departments have also adopted AI-powered report writing tools to generate police reports from body-worn camera audio (see MPR News, "An AI tool is generating police reports for some Minnesota departments, prompting legal concerns," Oct. 8, 2025, https://www.mprnews.org/story/2025/10/08/minnesota-police-departments-using-ai-to-generate-reports). A League of Minnesota Cities publication encouraged city workers to use tools like Copilot and ChatGPT to jumpstart drafts and complete tasks more efficiently (see League of Minnesota Cities, "Shaping the Future of AI in Your City," https://www.lmc.org/news-publications/magazine/may-jun-2024/ai-in-your-city/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot Chat interaction data within the Microsoft 365 GCC environment may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. Copilot usage reports and activity data can be accessed via the Microsoft 365 admin center under Reports > Usage. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The Minnesota Government Data Practices Act covers all "government data," defined as all data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity regardless of physical form. Minn. Stat. § 13.02, subd. 7. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has issued guidance requiring that AI-generated content used in official business be treated as government data subject to existing retention schedules. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Hello,
The City of Minneapolis accepts requests for data through our online portal here: https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal
Once you register you can submit your request using specific forms to more accurately route your request, check on the status of your request, and receive your responsive data all within the portal.
If you would prefer not to use the portal you can call 311  (612-673-3000) and they will submit a request on your behalf. You will still need to provide contact information or set up a way for us to provide you with the data.
You can also request your report at the Public Service Building in downtown Minneapolis.
If you have any questions, or experience any difficulties using the portal, please let us know.
More information about data practices, city policy, and other frequently asked questions is available here: http://www.minneapolismn.gov/datapractices/index.htm


Katie Knudsen l Enterprise Information Management Analyst l City of Minneapolis – Office of City Clerk l Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov<mailto:Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov>

communications.subject: Welcome to the City of Minneapolis OpenCity Portal
communications.communication:
Hi 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com, Thank you for registering with OpenCity, the City of Minneapolis portal for public records requests. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal) Your user name: 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com 
 If you need to reset your password, please go to OpenCity and click the “Forgot Password” link in the top right corner to be emailed a new password. Learn About Data Practices Our OpenCity portal includes an Knowledge Base with articles about how to submit an effective data request, answers to frequently asked questions, and information about data practices at the City. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal?id=kb_view2) 
 Available Data To explore data already published online from the City of Minneapolis, please visit the Minneapolis Open Government web page. (http://www.minneapolismn.gov/government/open/index.htm) 
 Creating a New Request Now that you have registered, you can log into the OpenCity portal to: 
 submit a request for data. check the status of your requests. communicate with the Data Practices team about a specific request. receive responsive data directly through the portal. 
 Please do not reply to this email. 
 Thank you, 
 OpenCity Data Practices Team ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov (mailto:ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov) 
 
Ref:MSG8854199

 How do I get updates about my request? We will provide updates by email. In addition, you can view the status updates of your requests on the OpenCity portal. communications.communication:
Hi 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com A comment has been added to your request: DR26_005787: Media - AI Chat data 
 Public Comments: 
2026-05-28 14:41:23 CDT - Katie Knudsen Public Comments Hello, Data responsive to items 1, 2, and 3 of your request are available in the OpenCity portal. We are still working on item 4. It can take up to 15 minutes for the data to appear. Please log in to your OpenCity account, click on My Requests and then select this reference number to view the available data. For item 1 of your request, we focused on Microsoft CoPilot. IT has let me know that ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Claude are not City of Minneapolis applications, so they cannot track or provide data from those applications. Some data for item 1 of your request was withheld pursuant to Minnesota State Statute 13.43. There are 2,090 additional documents remaining for item 1 of you request. If you would like to continue to receive data please reply in the OpenCity portal. If I do not hear from you by Thursday, June 4 your request will be closed. Thank you, Katie Knudsen l Enterprise Information Management Analyst City of Minneapolis – Office of City Clerk l Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov (mailto:Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov) To reply to this comment, you may respond to this email or login to the OpenCity portal and add comments to have your response added to your request. Data responsive to your request DR26_005787 is now available on the OpenCity Portal. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal) You may access your data from the OpenCity Portal. communications.subject: OpenCity data request DR26_005787 - Your request has been resolved
communications.communication:
Hi 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com 
Contact us 
 This resolved request will remain under My Open Requests on the OpenCity Portal for two weeks. During that time, you may contact us regarding your request through the portal, or by replying to this email. 
 Closed requests After two weeks, your request will move to My Closed Requests on the OpenCity Portal. You will be able to see your request for 60 days; however, you will no longer be able to contact us regarding this request in the portal. Once your request is closed you will need to contact us at ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov. You must include your reference number, and if possible, forward this email with your communication. We will respond to you as soon as we can. (mailto:ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov) 
 Thank you,

Hello, Since you haven't contacted us about receiving more data, your request will be closed. If you are still interested in receiving data for this subject, please submit a new request, and we will be happy to continue sending you data. Thank you, Katie Knudsen l Enterprise Information Management Analyst   City of Minneapolis – City Clerk's Office l katherine.knudsen@minneapolismn.gov she/her 
2026-05-28 14:41:23 CDT - Katie Knudsen Public Comments Hello, Data responsive to items 1, 2, and 3 of your request are available in the OpenCity portal. We are still working on item 4. It can take up to 15 minutes for the data to appear. Please log in to your OpenCity account, click on My Requests and then select this reference number to view the available data. For item 1 of your request, we focused on Microsoft CoPilot. IT has let me know that ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Claude are not City of Minneapolis applications, so they cannot track or provide data from those applications. Some data for item 1 of your request was withheld pursuant to Minnesota State Statute 13.43. There are 2,090 additional documents remaining for item 1 of you request. If you would like to continue to receive data please reply in the OpenCity portal. If I do not hear from you by Thursday, June 4 your request will be closed. Thank you, Katie Knudsen l Enterprise Information Management Analyst City of Minneapolis – Office of City Clerk l Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov (mailto:Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov) To reply to this comment, you may respond to this email or login to the OpenCity portal and add comments to have your response added to your request. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal) Thank you, 
Data Requested: Required: Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs April 9, 2026 Data Practices Responsible Authority City of Minneapolis 350 S. Fifth Street, Room 301 Minneapolis, MN 55415 Dear Data Practices Responsible Authority, I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work. We are writing to request records related to the City of Minneapolis's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Microsoft Copilot. Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, Minn. Stat. § 13.03, we are requesting the following records: 1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City of Minneapolis employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV). 2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing City of Minneapolis employee use of Microsoft Copilot or other generative AI chatbot tools. 3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data. 4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees, including the Minneapolis Police Department. We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses). Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has deployed AI assistants to more than 22,000 state employees and approved Microsoft Copilot Chat as a tool for handling non-public data within the Microsoft Government Community Cloud (GCC) environment (see MNIT, "2025 Annual Report," https://mn.gov/mnit/about-mnit/legislative-reports/2025-annual-report.jsp; see also MnDOT, "Generative Artificial Intelligence Standards," https://www.dot.state.mn.us/policy/it-data/it-003.html) (https://www.dot.state.mn.us/policy/it-data/it-003.html%29) The City of Minneapolis, including the Minneapolis Police Department, has reportedly generated over 37,000 items using Copilot. Minnesota police departments have also adopted AI-powered report writing tools to generate police reports from body-worn camera audio (see MPR News, "An AI tool is generating police reports for some Minnesota departments, prompting legal concerns," Oct. 8, 2025, https://www.mprnews.org/story/2025/10/08/minnesota-police-departments-using-ai-to-generate-reports) A League of Minnesota Cities publication encouraged city workers to use tools like Copilot and ChatGPT to jumpstart drafts and complete tasks more efficiently (see League of Minnesota Cities, "Shaping the Future of AI in Your City," https://www.lmc.org/news-publications/magazine/may-jun-2024/ai-in-your-city/) We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files. (https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files) The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20. I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below. Thank you for your time and assistance. Sincerely, Alexander Spangher Postdoctoral Researcher Stanford University alexspan@stanford.edu P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot Chat interaction data within the Microsoft 365 GCC environment may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. Copilot usage reports and activity data can be accessed via the Microsoft 365 admin center under Reports > Usage. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful. P.P.S. The Minnesota Government Data Practices Act covers all "government data," defined as all data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity regardless of physical form. Minn. Stat. § 13.02, subd. 7. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has issued guidance requiring that AI-generated content used in official business be treated as government data subject to existing retention schedules. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2276019/ 
From Date: 2025-01-01 
To Date: 2026-04-28 
: Minneapolis, Minnesota City Clerk of Minneapolis, MN.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: school theatre
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public reports show that this school district has been subject to theatrical plays, musicals, and student performances: 
https://www.onstageblog.com/editorials/2024/11/16/school-district-cancels-high-school-play-after-obsencity-complaints

Therefore, I request the following: 
1) All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials (including recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides formats), and other guidance related to “Dog Sees God: Confessions of a Teenage Blockhead,” “parent complaints,” or obscenity. This includes A) any notes taken during meetings that discuss plays, musicals, or student performances B) any other document that has been used to help explain plays, musicals, and student performances, including internal documentation, public relations materials, and executive summaries.
2) Any digital communications (including but not limited to emails and text messages) as well as documents, (including but not limited to PDF, word processing, excel, and slide documents) about, pertaining to, or mentioning “Dog Sees God”, “parent complaints”, “Peanuts”, “obscenity”, or “offensive”.
3) Any emails, presentations, videos, memos, recordings, or other records mentioning theatrical plays, musicals, and student performances.
Please perform this search for records between Jan 1, 2024 and the time this request is processed. This request is not for commercial purposes and will be used in the course of reporting for 404 Media, an independent, journalist-owned news site (www.404media.co).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Koebler

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Data Retention Policies: Any policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), or retention schedules specifying how long captured license plate data, images, and metadata are stored by the agency before deletion.

2. Data Sharing Agreements: A list, log, ledger, or copy of active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) detailing all outside agencies, law enforcement entities, or third parties with whom the Peachtree City Police Department shares its Flock Safety / ALPR data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Buchanan

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: N019538-050526
communications.communication:
Dear Miriam : 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019538-050526 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

All emails to/from Trista Harper from March 5, 2025 through March 8, 2025. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2281446/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019538-050526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
June 5, 2026 Miriam Bhimani 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019538-050526 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: All emails to/from Trista Harper from March 5, 2025 through March 8, 2025. RESPONSE: Records responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center. Please visit the link and enter your username and password to access the records. http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx .. The safety and security of students and staff are of the utmost importance and the records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(v):Vulnerability assessments, security measures, and response policies or plans that are designed to identify, prevent, or respond to potential attacks upon a community’s population or systems, facilities, or installations, the destruction or contamination of which would constitute a clear and present danger to the health or safety of the community, but only to the extent that disclosure could reasonably be expected to jeopardize the effectiveness of the measures or the safety of the personnel who implement them or the public. Information exempt under this item may include such things as details pertaining to the mobilization or deployment of personnel or equipment, to the operations of communication systems or protocols, or to tactical operations. Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(f) exempts any “Preliminary drafts, notes, recommendations, memoranda and other records in which opinions are expressed, or policies or actions are formulated, except that a specific record or relevant portion of a record shall not be exempt when the record is publicly cited and identified by the head of the public body.” Please be advised that student information is exempt under FOIA and records have been redacted pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, section 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Google meeting links have been redacted as they are exempt pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(u): Information that would disclose or might lead to the disclosure of secret or confidential information, codes, algorithms, programs, or private keys intended to be used to create electronic or digital signatures under the Electronic Commerce Security Act. Redactions have been made pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(b), “Private information, unless disclosure is required by another provision of this Act, a State or federal law or a court order,” and  5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), “Personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs the legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. The disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees and officials shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Illinois state law prohibits the release of portions of public employee personnel files, such as teacher performance evaluations. Per 105 ILCS 5/24A-7.1, “Disclosure of public school teacher, principal, and superintendent performance evaluations is prohibited,” and 820 ILCS 40/11, “Disclosure of performance evaluations under the Freedom of Information Act shall be prohibited,” and related documents have been redacted. Section 7(1)(d)(iv) of the FOIA allows a public to withhold records/information that would unavoidably reveal the identity of a complainant or witness. Lastly, pursuant to Section 7.5(h) of the FOIA, exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706.  You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.  Thank you for your request. Sincerely, Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Materials related to and sufficient to show all instances of alleged or actual inappropriate or illegal behavior or actions resulting in compensation or expenditures involving this agency. 

Responsive materials include records of all settlements (formal and informal) and payments to which this agency was or is a party for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed. Responsive materials include those for matters and legal claims resolved prior to the initiation of any litigation. 

In particular, materials responsive to this request include all reports, lists, databases, spreadsheets, and other documentation detailing incidents of legal claims or other allegations requiring compensation for claimants, attorneys, or any other party. 

If spreadsheets or similar documents are available in response to this request, please provide all segregable data, including but not limited to:

The nature of the claim or legal matter
The claimant and other parties involved
The relevant case and tracking numbers for court and internal systems
The date the lawsuit was served or litigation commenced
The final disposition or current status of the case, including whether a case is still pending
The amount spent to date as a result of the legal engagement
The amount paid to any parties related to the legal claim, including any city payout and any non-monetary compensation

If clarification of this request is required, please respond to the provided email address or leave a message at 617-299-1832.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Capitola Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain “@flocksafety.com,” between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

This request includes:
- Emails where @flocksafety.com appears in the sender, recipient (To, CC, BCC), or within the body of the message
- Attachments of any file type, including but not limited to PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, and images
- Calendar invitations, meeting requests, and related responses involving Flock Safety personnel

Please search all agency-controlled email systems and accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including individual officer accounts, group mailboxes, and archived or backup systems. Records should be provided in native electronic format where possible (e.g., .pst, .msg, or .eml with metadata preserved), including all available metadata fields such as sender, recipients, timestamps, and subject lines.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Request for Public Records # 26-124
communications.communication:
The City of Capitola ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 ("Request 26-124").

The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.). Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort.  The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request.

The City has reviewed its records inventory and identified records responsive to your request. The City is reviewing records for responsiveness and privilege.  Review is ongoing.  The City will endeavor to provide responsive records on or before May 14, 2026.  If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please email me.

Sincerely,

Julia Gautho, CMC
City Clerk
jgautho@ci.capitola.ca.us<mailto:jgautho@ci.capitola.ca.us>
(831) 475-7300 x. 228

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #26-124
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

The City of Capitola (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 (“Request”), seeking “all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain ‘@flocksafety.com,’ between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.”

The City wishes to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act. Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs, or reports in response to a request.

On April 30, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request.  The City now provides the initial batch of records responsive to your Request through the following link: https://bwslaw.sharefile.com/d-s9153de22c0ad4fa6950417c37938bf15

The City withheld any responsive law enforcement records pursuant to Government Code section 7923.600.  The City also withheld certain records pursuant to California Civil Code section 1798.90.55(b) (See also Cal. Gov. Code § 7927.705.).  The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000).

The City is in the process of reviewing records for responsiveness and privilege. The City will endeavor to provide an additional production of responsive records on or before May 28, 2026.  If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@ci.capitola.ca.us.

Julia Gautho, CMC
City Clerk

communications.subject: Public Record Request #26-124
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
The City of Capitola (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 (“Request”), seeking “all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain ‘@flocksafety.com,’ between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.”
The City wishes to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act. Under established California law, the City of obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs, or reports in response to a request.
On April 30, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request. On May 14, 2026, the City provided the first batch of records responsive to your Request. The City now provides the second batch of records responsive to your Request through the following link:
https://bwslaw.sharefile.com/public/share/webs7d5e379090154dbe9372bef15ab3b9c6
The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000.) The City redacted personal contact information as the public interest served in withholding personal contact information clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure, to prevent an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000; See also: Cal. Const., art. 1 § 1.).
The City withheld certain confidential financial information pursuant to Government Code section 7922.000. The City also withheld records that are draft documents not maintained in the ordinary course of business and/or that may reveal deliberative process of the City staff. Cal. Government Code §§ 7927.500, 7922.000; Citizens for a Better Environment v. California Department of Food and Agriculture (1985) 171 Cal.App.3d 704; The Humane Society of the United States v. Superior Court of Yolo County (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 1233.
With this letter and production, the City considers your Request closed. If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@cityofcapitola.gov
Sincerely,


Rosie Wyatt

Deputy City Clerk

831.475.7300 x 206
