communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all records concerning the department's acquisition, deployment, and utilization of Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs) and Flock Safety technology. 

This request includes, but is not limited to:
1. Current policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and data retention guidelines regarding ALPR/Flock Safety usage.
2. The current total number of active ALPR/Flock Safety cameras operated or accessed by the department.
3. Any active contracts, service agreements, or memoranda of understanding (MOUs) with Flock Safety or neighboring jurisdictions regarding data sharing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Buchanan

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records (Statesboro Police Department)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records Officer,

I am writing to formally amend the scope of my Georgia Open Records Act request submitted earlier today regarding the Flock Safety ALPR system. Please include the following operational data logs in the final fulfillment of this request:

1. Master System Audit Logs (Organizational/Network Audit Logs): Please provide the complete user access history logs for the specific date range of April 1, 2026, through April 30, 2026. This data must include the user IDs/officer logins, exact timestamps, and the mandatory "stated justification" text reasons typed in by officers to execute system searches.

Fee Safe-Harbor and Rolling Production:
If extracting these specific user audit logs will require administrative labor that results in a fee, please treat this as a rolling production. I request that the static documents from my original message (such as contracts and policies) be released immediately at no cost. Please notify me with an itemized estimate and obtain my explicit authorization before performing any paid labor that would exceed a total of $25.00.

Thank you,
Paul Buchanan

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records (Statesboro Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good Morning,
We responded to your previous request on May20th at 8:47 am stating, we
cannot fulfill this request internally. We sent a help desk ticket to
FUSUS, but they stated they could not fulfill the request due to
insufficient staffing to handle the time required.
Thanks,

*Robin Demshar*
*Records Manager*
*City of Statesboro*
*50 East Main Street*
*Statesboro, GA 30458*

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records (Statesboro Police Department)
communications.communication:
Dear Records Manager Demshar,

Thank you for your response. While I understand that the Statesboro Police Department utilizes a third-party vendor (FUSUS) to manage these systems, I must respectfully clarify the department's obligations under the Georgia Open Records Act (GORA).

1. Third-Party Records are Public Records Under Georgia law ($O.C.G.A. \ \S \ 50-18-70(b)(2)$), public records include all documents and data received or maintained by a private vendor on behalf of a public agency. A public agency cannot deny or defer an Open Records request based on a third-party contractor's internal staffing shortages or administrative constraints. The legal obligation to ensure public access remains entirely with the City of Statesboro.

2. Immediate Release of Static Documents As noted in my amended request on May 26th, I explicitly requested a rolling production. The "insufficient staffing" argument cited by FUSUS regarding time-intensive data log extraction does not apply to the static documents requested in my original submission. Please immediately release the following existing, static records, which require no complex extraction:
-Current policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and data retention guidelines regarding ALPR/Flock Safety/FUSUS usage.
-Active contracts, service agreements, or memoranda of understanding (MOUs) with Flock Safety, FUSUS, or neighboring jurisdictions.

3. Narrowed Scope Compromise for Data Logs To accommodate the administrative limitations mentioned by your vendor, I am willing to significantly narrow the scope of the Master System Audit Logs. Instead of the full month of April, please provide the user access history logs for just a single week: April 1, 2026, through April 7, 2026.If fulfilling this narrowed log request will still incur an administrative fee, please provide an itemized cost estimate before proceeding, as specified under GORA. Thank you for your assistance in ensuring compliance with Georgia open government standards. I look forward to receiving the static policies and contracts while we resolve the log production.

Sincerely,

Paul Buchanan

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records (Statesboro Police Department)
communications.communication:
Dear Records Manager Demshar,

Thank you for your response. To clarify, my request is specifically for FLOCK data.

I understand you mentioned not seeing the amendment from May 26th. To ensure we are on the same page and to make this as straightforward as possible for your office to process, please fulfill the request by providing the existing, static documents first:

Policies & SOPs: Current operating procedures and data retention guidelines regarding your use of the Flock Safety ALPR system.

Contracts & MOUs: Active service agreements or data-sharing memoranda of understanding with Flock Safety or neighboring jurisdictions regarding Flock data.

Regarding the operational data logs: as noted in my narrowed amendment on June 1st, please provide the system audit/access logs (including user logins and stated search justifications) strictly for the first week of April (April 1, 2026, through April 7, 2026) specifically for the Flock Safety system.

If your office prefers to handle this track independently to make obtaining the records easier, please release the static contracts and policies immediately while we finalize any administrative fee estimates for this single week of Flock audit logs.

Thank you for your ongoing assistance,
Paul Buchanan

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records (Statesboro Police Department)
communications.communication:
Dear Records Manager Demshar,

Thank you for providing the SOPs and policies.

To clarify, while your response noted that the contract agreements are exempt under O.C.G.A. § 35-1-22(f), that specific technical exemption does not apply to the Master System Audit Logs / User Access History that I requested. As a reminder, I narrowed this request down to just a single week of data: April 1, 2026, through April 7, 2026.

The public release of basic user logs—showing officer IDs, exact timestamps, and the typed-out search justifications—does not expose "data system secrets" or compromise the technical security architecture of the network. These are standard records of public employee system utilization and are subject to disclosure under the Georgia Open Records Act.

Please confirm if the department is explicitly denying the production of these narrowed user access logs, or provide the itemized administrative fee estimate required to extract them if it will exceed $25.00.

Thank you for your time and assistance,

Paul Buchanan

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The Compensation Agreement between the tenant, developer, and authority and Teays Valley Local Schools which provides for the School District to receive certain compensation in exchange for the tax revenue foregone by the School District as a result of the Community Reinvestment Area Exemptions granted by the Pickaway County Agreement for the Anduril Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility project.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes

communications.communication:
Hi Trey,

I really appreciate your providing me with this agreement. May I ask two follow up questions?

One, pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, may I have a copy of the semi-annual PILOT Statement produced between January 1- March 1 of this year that specifies that the amount due for the first half of the annual Project Thor PILOT payment to the Teays Valley Local School District? If Teays Valley produced any objections to the calculation, could you kindly provide me with the objection?

Also, may I ask if you all have estimated what your revenue loss will be over 30 years due to the compensation agreement equaling 30% of the portion of real property taxes Teays Valley would have received had the CRA Exemption not been in place for the Arsenal-1 buildings? Ie, what amount does Teays Valley estimate that the full 100% would have been?

I really appreciate the consideration -- thank you very much. 

best

Taylor Barnes

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any memos, emails, and/or policies since January 20, 2025, related to the effort between ATF, DEA, FBI, US Marshals, and HSI to locate and interview at-risk unaccompanied alien children who entered the United States over the past four years.

- Any memos, reports, and/or emails drafted since January 20, 2025 quantifying the number and/or percentage of children identified as abused, trafficked, neglected, or exploited through Unaccompanied Alien Children Operations and/or other HSI wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children. 

- All trainings, memos, and/or policies used since January 20, 2025 about communicating with children and/or using trauma-informed interviewing skills provided to agents completing wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lara S Nochomovitz

communications.subject: eFOIA Request Received
communications.communication:
# Individual Information

Prefix First Name Middle Name Last Name Suffix Email Phone Location

# Domestic Address

Address Line 1 Address Line 2 City State Postal

# Agreement to Pay

How you will pay

I am requesting a fee waiver for my request and have reviewed the FOIA
reference guide. If my fee waiver is denied, I would like to limit my request
to the two free hours of searching and 100 duplicated pages.

# Proof Of Affiliation for Fee Waiver

Waiver Explanation

    
    
    I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have
    previously written
    about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000
    readers in outlets such
    as Gizmodo, MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, Unicorn Riot, and
    The Outline,
    among others. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and
    my editorial and
    writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on
    the files online and
    make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive
    and the journalist
    non-profit MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my
    research is not limited
    to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and
    attitudes of the government
    itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of
    this particular subject in
    advance.
    Further, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the
    released information to a
    broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v.
    Dep’t of Justice, 365
    F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.
    3d 807, 814-15 (2d
    Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had
    “firm” plans to “publish a
    number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and
    national security policy."
    Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.
    Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the
    nature of the requester,
    not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a
    representative of the news
    media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a
    representative of the
    news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which
    he or she is requesting
    information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the
    purposes of determining
    the appropriate fee category.
    As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting
    on it and making
    the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.
    This request specifically includes both previously processed and previously
    unprocessed
    documents. If the documents are 25 or more years old and previously
    processed documents were
    processed before the 25th anniversary of the document’s creation, I request
    that in addition to the
    previous processed copy, the document be reprocessed and redactions
    reviewed.

Documentation Files

# Deceased Individual

Prefix First Name Middle Name Last Name Suffix ARN SSN FBI Number Date of
Birth Place of Birth Date of Death Additional Information

    
    
    To Whom It May Concern:
    
    Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following
    records:
    
    Records mentioning or describing Robert Altman.
    
    The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and
    this request is not being made for commercial purposes.
    
    In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me
    of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the
    request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM
    if not.
    
    Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look
    forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days,
    as the statute requires.
    
    Sincerely,
    
    Emma North-Best
    
    Attachments:
    
    
    https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/EmmaBest/93227/Robert20Altman2C20Video20Game20Mogul20Who20Survived20Scandal2C20Dies20at2073.pdf
    
    https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/EmmaBest/93227/Fee20category20and20search20parameters.pdf
    Upload documents directly:
    https://https://www.muckrock.comhttps://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Ffederal-bureau-of-investigation-10%252Frobert-altman-108100%252F%253Femail%253Defoia%252540subscriptions.fbi.gov&url_auth_token=AAAj2O3FGiSDXH3mORgxvQEAgVs%3A1lBWPW%3A2GERi61X5ellooPDsSuskrfo8is

Supporting documentation **

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

** 

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1489901-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 104 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 853 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,786 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,500 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

COVID-19 Impact: RIDS resumed full staffing on April 12, 2021.  Commensurate with the return of full staffing levels, RIDS will being increasing its FOIA processing production.  While RIDS will make its best efforts to meet increased production goals, this increased production rate may be negatively impacted by COVID-19 workplace disruptions.  As a result, estimated dates of completion for Freedom of Information Act requests were impacted and you can expect lengthened response timelines.  Please note that these are estimates, and these timelines may change as this national emergency evolves.  The FBI remains committed to fulfilling your FOIPA request in the timeliest manner possible.   Thank you for your patience and understanding.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI RS 4.10.26
communications.communication:
Dear FOIA Officer:

You are receiving this correspondence as a request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. subsection 552. I am requesting the following:

Copies of all Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) records, documents, and communications responsive to Colonel Raymond Starrett Sleeper, aka Raymond Starrat Sleeper, aka Raymond Sterritt Sleeper, aka Raymond S. Sleeper (September 13-1914-April 14, 1995; SSN 001-14-5228).

Col. Sleeper served as the Commanding Officer of the United States Air Force's Foreign Technology Division (FTD), the Deputy Chief of the Strategic Vulnerability Branch in its Air Intelligence Division, and faculty at the Air War College. Additionally, he maintained personal affiliations with the Institute for American Strategy, later known as the American Security Council Foundation, and the Western Goals Foundation.

Col. Sleeper wrote on the subject of Soviet psychological warfare, publishing books like "A Lexicon of Marxist-Leninist Semantics" and "Mesmerized by the Bear: The Soviet of Strategy of Deception".

Proof of death is not required as Col. Sleeper's date of birth was over 100 years ago; however, his gravestone is listed on a public database, Find a Grave, and can be found here: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/149575947/raymond-starrett-sleeper

I am asking you waive any fees associated with this request. I am a member of the news media. My bylines, which include Unicorn Riot, Covert Action Magazine, and the Geopolitical Economy Report, can be found in both print and electronic publications, and I am sending this request in coordination with Muckrock, an award-winning and nationally-recognized news organization. Please take note of the Office of Management and Budget guidelines published March 27, 1987 (52 FR 10012) that include electronic publications and other nontraditional publishers as representatives of the news media.

A fee waiver is appropriate in situations where "disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government, and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester,” 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(iii). While with the FTD, Col. Sleeper was briefly in charge of overseeing the United States Air Force's Project Blue Book, a study into unidentified flying objects (UFOs). Historical disclosures by the United States Air Force and Central Intelligence Agency indicate programs like Project Blue Book were used to deceive the American public about the existence (or non-existence) of UFOs. A 1994 report issued by the Air Force, "The Roswell Report: Case Closed", states that a 1947 UFO crash near Roswell, New Mexico, was in fact a "compartmented, sensitive effort" involving balloon reconnaissance research named Project MOGUL, according to pages 24 through 29. A copy can be found here:  https://www.dafhistory.af.mil/Portals/16/documents/AFD-101201-038.pdf

Germane to Project Blue Book, a 1997 article in the Central Intelligence Agency's "Studies in Intelligence" journal titled "CIA's Role in the Study of UFO's, 1947-1990" observes collaboration between agency officials and Blue Book investigators. Gerald Haines, the article's author, writes on page 73: "The early U-2s were silver (they were later painted black) and reflected the rays from the sun, especially at sunrise and sunset. They often appeared as fiery objects to observers below. Air Force BLUE BOOK investigators aware of the secret U-2 flights tried to explain away such sightings by linking them to natural phenomena such as ice crystals and temperature inversions. By checking with the Agency's U-2 Project Staff in Washington, BLUE BOOK investigators were able to attribute many UFO sightings to U-2 flights. They were careful, however, not to reveal the true cause of the sighting to the public." A copy of the article can be found here: https://www.cia.gov/resources/csi/static/cia-role-study-UFOs.pdf

Attached is correspondence, in the form of a letter taken from Project Blue Book's archives, verifying Col. Sleeper's connection to the program. As Col. Sleeper was involved with Project Blue Book and both the Air Force and CIA have acknowledged this program and others like it were used to deceive the American public, any records generated by the FBI about Col. Sleeper would contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government as it relates to its research into UFOs and other relevant subject areas. The letter can also be found here: https://www.fold3.com/image/7282652/tucson-arizona-blank-page-8-us-project-blue-book-ufo-investigations-1947-1969

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. All documents will be provided to the general public without charge.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Records System, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for all known identities for Col. Sleeper in both main-file records and cross-reference records related to the Human Resources Research Institute, Institute for American Strategy, American Security Council Foundation, and Western Goals Foundation.

In the event that there are fees, I am willing to pay up to $100. Please advise if fees will exceed this amount so I might make an arrangement for requesting the records. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I look forward to your response within 20 working days, as the statute requires. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Sincerely,

Robert Skvarla, Jr.

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: FW: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)
communications.communication:
Good morning Alexander,

Your FOIA request for the electronic Assessment records has been received.

In order to fulfill your request, we need some additional information please.

Are you requesting the 2026 or 2027 electronic assessment records?  Currently, we are in the 2026 assessing database and after spring equalization is approved in just a few days, the system will be rolled over to 2027.  The 2027 assessment records should be available to be sent to you during the week of April 20th.

Also, the county's assessing data is available in ASCII format and can be emailed to you.  Because of the electronic format, you have the option of paying for the export through a regular data request from our office; or you can request it through FOIA and pay additional fees for preparation of the export.  If you choose to continue this request as a FOIA request, I'll provide to you the contact information for our department's FOIA Coordinator.

Our County Commissioners have set the fee of our assessing data exports at $0.50 per parcel.  Currently, there are 16,515 real, personal and exempt parcels for Missaukee County. The total cost for an electronic assessment data export in ASCII format emailed to you would be $8,257.50, if the request is done through our office and is a regular request for the export.  If you choose to continue with a FOIA request, the cost is $8,257.50 plus any applicable FOIA fees.

Once we receive payment, we would email you the export at the email address you provide.

Please respond with your preference of type of request and year of assessment data requested and I will give you the total amount due and payment instructions.

If you have further questions, please feel free to contact me.

Have a good day,
Sandy Moffit
Data Coordinator
Missaukee County Equalization
PO Box 800
Lake City MI  49651
(231) 839-4967 ext. 212

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)
communications.communication:
Hi Sandy,

Thank you for getting back to me regarding my request.

To answer your first question regarding assessment year: 2025 data would be fine, but it would also be helpful for us to have historic data earlier than that, if it's available.

And then to answer your second question, unfortunately paying over $8,000 for each year exceeds what FOIA allows.

I understand that Missaukee Co may have enacted an ordinance or policy which may conflict with the fee structure FOIA requires under state law. I am willing to pay the county's costs under FOIA, but not thousands of dollars in addition to that cost.

This may need to be something we review together under the appeals process if Missaukee's policy is to charge above and beyond what the Act provides.

For Missaukee's FOIA appeals process, does the county utilize an administrative appeal (ie, I speak to a county chair or administrator) or will I be able to present at your next Board of Commissioners meeting (as I did in Livingston Co)?

Thank you again for your help with this.

communications.communication:
Good morning Sheila,

Thank you for getting back to me but could you also share with me how Missaukee arrived at the $8200 fee?

I looked into FOIA's exemptions list in MCL15.243, many things appear to be exempt but I don't see the county's property tax records being listed there. And then I see the fee limitations described in 15.234 so I'm confused how the $8200 is added on top of the FOIA fee (I can understand and agree to an incremental based charge set up like the FOIA part of the fee)?

Thanks again for your help with this.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

The fee for the Assessing Data Export was established by the Missaukee County Board of Commissioners at $0.50 per parcel and is included in the Revenue Policy Fee Schedule (Policy F‑18400), most recently adopted on March 11, 2025.

The Assessing Data Export is provided as a digital file in ASCII format and contains county assessing information, including parcel numbers, owner names and mailing addresses, legal descriptions, assessed and taxable values, property sale and deed history, and other miscellaneous parcel-related data. This export is not billed on a per‑page basis.

Please let me know if you have any additional questions or require further clarification. Otherwise, I will assume this information addresses your inquiry. If you wish to proceed with your request, the data will be forwarded to you upon receipt of payment.


Sheila L. Morgan
FOIA Coordinator
PO Box 348
Lake City, MI 49651
231-839-3111

The county's assessing data is available in ASCII format and can be emailed to you.  Our County Commissioners have set the fee of our assessing data exports at $0.50 per parcel. Currently, there are 16,515 real, personal and exempt parcels for Missaukee County. The total cost for an electronic assessment data export in ASCII format emailed to you would be $8,262.76 including the FOIA fee.


Sheila L. Morgan

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Missaukee County)
communications.communication:
Ok, thank you Sheila. Ok -- so this is something like 22 or 23 years of property tax value (SEV/TV) data from Equalization for $8,262.76

This will be the figure I appeal, but if I am incorrect in my understanding on the grand total for all years, please let me know -- since it's important for me to have the history for our educational references to contextualize the uncapping which happens at the point of qualified sales. If you've ever heard someone say "I had no idea how much my taxes would go up" <------- that is what we'll explain with the information you are requiring $8,262.76 for.

Thanks again for your help, and I'll get back to you with that appeal soon.

Could you please send me your agreements with the data brokers (Zillow, Corelogic/Cotality, etc)? 

Emails and invoices would be fine if there are no licenses or sales agreements in place.

Please consider this a new and distinct FOIA request, since it concerns different records than the assessor's tax rolls -- I am curious and a bit surprised this process has taken almost 2 months.

Thank you,
Alexander Munro

Hello Sheila,

Thank you for sending the County's FOIA Policy and the letter to muckrock.

Re Muckrock, please note that my 2 requests were on behalf of myself, Alexander Munro. Muckrock is a nonprofit online FOIA platform (comparable to justFOIA or NextRequest) and I am neither employed by nor affiliated with Muckrock. I use Muckrock for tracking purposes, but if you were to upload the responsive records via Muckrock (via email ending w/ muckrock.com) that should ensure them to be publicly searchable for no cost -- I only say that incase Missaukee would appreciate that, alternatively you can always email me personally at foia@alexandermunro.com

Are you going to send over the Zillow agreements and correspondence? 

I mentioned that as a separate FOIA since it concerns a different type of record (agreements and correspondence w/ the data brokers). I would like to understand the impasse Missaukee and I are at with regards to my earlier FOIA which was denied -- if Missaukee intends to deny both of my requests, I would appreciate some direction in terms of the appeals process for Missaukee FOIA denials.

Thank you for all of your help with this. Feel free to call if you have any issues or if there's anything I can help with.

Alexander Munro
34 N Huron St OFC, Ypsilanti MI 48197

734-272-6612

title: Select KHC LIHTC and TEB lists (2017-2025)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Select KHC LIHTC and TEB lists (2017-2025)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

First, applications lists (PDFs) for the following two 9% LIHTC competitive rounds:
- 2018 Competitive Application Round for Housing Credit and Non-Credit Resources
- 2019 Competitive Application Round for Housing Credit and KHC Gap Resources

Second, applications lists (PDFs) and awards  lists (AKA "funded report" or "funded projects" PDFs) for the following Tax Exempt Bond (TEB) rounds, as specified in the brackets:
- 2017 NOFA for Gap Financing for Tax Exempt Bonds [applications & awards]
- 2019 Tax-Exempt Bond NOFA [applications & awards]
- 2020 Tax-Exempt Bonds with Gap Financing NOFA [applications]
- 2022 NOFA for Tax-Exempt Bonds with Gap Financing [applications & awards]
- 2023 Tax-Exempt Bond with KHC Gap Financing NOFA [applications]
- 2025 Tax-Exempt Bond Only NOFA [awards]

For reference, regarding the two housing credit application lists, see the 2026 equivalent at https://www.kyhousing.org/Partners/Developers/Multifamily/Documents/2026%20Housing%20Credit%20Application%20Submissions.pdf.

For reference, regarding the TEB-related records, see the following from 2024:
- Equivalent applications list: https://www.kyhousing.org/Partners/Developers/Multifamily/Documents/2024%20Tax-Exempt%20Bond%20Only%20NOFA%20Applications%20Submitted.pdf
- Equivalent awards list: https://www.kyhousing.org/Partners/Developers/Multifamily/Documents/2024%20Tax-Exempt%20Bond%20Only%20NOFA%20Funded%20Applications.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lars Brown

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Hollis

communications.subject: RE: 166-2026 Public Records Request: Flock Records
communications.communication:
Good morning,

The Police Department does not have any records responsive to your request regarding Flock Safety.
The Flock Safety system is maintained through a subscription and contractual agreement with Flock Safety. Flock Safety is responsible for the collection, storage, maintenance, and retention of any records generated by its systems in accordance with its policies.
Information that may be responsive to your request can be accessed through the Flock Safety Transparency Portal at https://transparency.flocksafety.com/blue-springs-mo-pd, which is also available through the City's website.
Please note that records older than 30 days will not be available, as Flock Safety permanently deletes data after 30 days in accordance with its retention policy.
This concludes your request. No fees have been assessed.

Thank you,

Holly Grant

Custodian of Police Records
Blue Springs Police Department

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
communications.communication:
Thank you for the update Mr. Daniel.

I will forward the information requested in the request directly.

communications.subject: FW: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
communications.communication:
Please see attached your open record request.   We will consider this open record request closed.



Thanks,
Melissa



On May 29, 2026:
Subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
Good afternoon,

With the understanding that we haven't heard back from you or received payment we will consider you no longer are requesting this information and will close this open record request.
Thanks,
Melissa Robertson
---

On May 19, 2026:
Subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
Good afternoon, Mr. Ortega
RE: Open Records Request
The cost associated with your open records request is $34.92 and can be paid by credit card, check, or exact cash.
Please let me know if you would like for me to proceed and how payment will be made for collection of these records.
Thank you,
Melissa Robertson
City Clerk
---

On May 18, 2026:
Subject: FW: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
Good afternoon,
The City of LaGrange has received your Open Records Request.
Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate department(s) for review and processing. Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, the City has three (3) business days to provide a response outlining the availability of the requested records, along with any applicable costs and/or the estimated timeframe for completion.
If you have any questions in the meantime, please feel free to contact me.
Sincerely,
Melissa Robertson
City Clerk
---

On May 17, 2026:
Subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: FOIA Request: Motorola Solutions
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Motorola Solutions that were active any time between 1/1/2021 and the date this request is received.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Motorola Solutions. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Ortega

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2305105/

Filed via MuckRock.com
E-mail (Preferred): 211395-37263941@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:211395-37263941@requests.muckrock.com>

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS
For mailed responses, please address (see note):
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 211395
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: VHDA Website LIHTC Application PDFs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All 4% LIHTC and 9% LIHTC application PDFs submitted in 2018. These were once listed on the applications archive page of the superseded vhda.com website. The LIHTC applications archive page on virginiahousing.com no longer hosts them.

For reference, the list of projects for which applications were posted under the year 2018 included the following:

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Lars Brown.

Chris Wickham

Richmond, VA 23220

Chris Wickham

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: VHDA Website LIHTC Application PDFs
communications.communication:
Dear Chris Wickham,

Thank you so much to you and the LIHTC team for making available all of these applications. Most of the records are exactly what I was looking for, but I wanted to ask about two things:

(1) The Massanutten Manor Res.app.pdf looks like it is supplementary material rather than the application itself.
(2) There are 7 application documents that are labeled 2017 instead of 2018. It's possible that these 2017 applications are the ones referenced on my list of 2018 files, either because the "2018" listing is ambiguous, or because these applications were granted for a year other than that for which they were submitted. However, it is also possible some projects submitted applications in both years, 2017 and 2018, in which case my request seeks the application submitted in 2018. Here is the subset in question:
i. Culpepper Garden I Reservation App.pdf [4% LIHTC]
ii. The Berkeley II - Reservation Application.pdf [4% LIHTC]
iii. Carlton Views - Reservation Application.pdf
iv. New Clay House II- Reservation App.pdf
v. New Manchester Flats V-9 - Reservation App.pdf
vi. Riverbend Apartments - Reservation App.pdf
vii. Townhomes at Warwick Place II ASH - 2 - Reservation App.pdf

Could you check that these are the intended records?

Thanks again.

title: FOIA Request: Privately Funded Water Filtration System at City-Owned Facility

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Privately Funded Water Filtration System at City-Owned Facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request is directed to the attention of Michelle Dewey, FOIA Coordinator, City of Adrian, in accordance with the City’s published FOIA Procedures and Guidelines.

I request copies of public records related to the privately funded water filtration system referenced in the City of Adrian’s May 22, 2026 public statement, which stated in part: “A water filtration system was installed recently at a City owned facility (not City Hall). The system was not paid for by the City or taxpayer dollars, it was privately paid for.”

I request the following records:

1. Any record identifying the City-owned facility where the water filtration system was installed, including the facility name, address, or department served. This includes any installation work order, facilities request, maintenance log, building maintenance record, plumbing permit, building permit, internal memo, or similar record identifying the location.

2. Any record identifying the individual, company, foundation, nonprofit, vendor, or other entity that donated, paid for, reimbursed, or otherwise funded the installation or purchase of the water filtration system. This includes any donation acknowledgment, gift letter, thank-you correspondence, invoice, receipt, proof of payment, reimbursement record, City Commission agenda item, consent agenda item, or internal approval record.

3. Any record identifying the make, model, manufacturer, capacity, filter media, treatment specifications, maintenance requirements, and contaminants or compounds the system is designed to remove. This includes any specification sheet, manufacturer brochure, installation manual, warranty document, vendor quote, invoice, or product literature.

4. Any agreement, authorization, approval, memo, correspondence, or internal communication from January 1, 2025 through May 31, 2026 concerning the acceptance, installation, funding, donation, ownership, maintenance, replacement filters, or use of the privately funded water filtration system referenced in the May 22, 2026 public statement.

Please include complete copies of responsive records, including all pages, attachments, enclosures, transmittal emails, routing records, and related packet materials where applicable. Where records exist electronically, I request that they be provided in their existing electronic format where reasonably available.

I request a waiver or reduction of fees under MCL 15.234 on the ground that disclosure of these records primarily benefits the general public rather than my commercial interest. The records concern a privately funded improvement to a City-owned facility and involve questions of public spending, private funding, public facilities, drinking water, and City operations that residents cannot fully evaluate from currently public sources.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption claimed under MCL 15.243 for each withheld record and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions as required by MCL 15.244.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Doe

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Privately Funded Water Filtration System at City-Owned Facility
communications.communication:
Good morning,

We have received your request, and I expect to have a response to you by the end of the day on Wednesday of this week.

Thank you,
Michelle Dewey

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Privately Funded Water Filtration System at City-Owned Facility
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Your request has been granted in part, and denied in part. Please see below:


1. Any record identifying the City-owned facility where the water filtration system was installed, including the facility name, address, or department served. This includes any installation work order, facilities request, maintenance log, building maintenance record, plumbing permit, building permit, internal memo, or similar record identifying the location. Granted in part - please refer to the attached work order. Denied in part - no other record was located.

2. Any record identifying the individual, company, foundation, nonprofit, vendor, or other entity that donated, paid for, reimbursed, or otherwise funded the installation or purchase of the water filtration system. This includes any donation acknowledgment, gift letter, thank-you correspondence, invoice, receipt, proof of payment, reimbursement record, City Commission agenda item, consent agenda item, or internal approval record. Denied as no records were located responsive to this request.

3. Any record identifying the make, model, manufacturer, capacity, filter media, treatment specifications, maintenance requirements, and contaminants or compounds the system is designed to remove. This includes any specification sheet, manufacturer brochure, installation manual, warranty document, vendor quote, invoice, or product literature. Granted in part – please refer to attached work order. Denied in part, as no other records were located in responsive to this request.

4. Any agreement, authorization, approval, memo, correspondence, or internal communication from January 1, 2025 through May 31, 2026 concerning the acceptance, installation, funding, donation, ownership, maintenance, replacement filters, or use of the privately funded water filtration system referenced in the May 22, 2026 public statement. Granted in part – please refer to attached work order. Denied in part, as no other records were located in responsive to this request.

There is no charge for the response provided. If you have further questions or need additional clarification, please feel free to contact me.

Should you wish to appeal any of the portions of your request that were denied, you may fill out the form at the link below or submit a written appeal.

https://cms8.revize.com/revize/adrianmi/Document%20Center/Department/Administration/FOIA/FOIA-Appeal-Form-To-Appeal-a-Denial-of-Records-2015.pdf?t=202401181343080.

Thank you,

Michelle Dewey

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request #26-6038
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern, 

Thank you for your response. However, HIDTA do not respond to public records requests, because they claim they are not government bodies and instead refer requesters to the originating agencies. 

As you know, ALPRs are of significant public interest and APD has represented to the public that there are "strict policies and guardrails for this use. Should your agency be sharing ALPR data with the National HIDTA LPR network, one would expect your agency to maintain a copy of the MOU governing criminal justice data and the sensitive data of residents. Otherwise, those claims are empty. 

I respectfully request that you search your records again for this important document, or otherwise confirm that your agency is not sharing ALPR data with HIDTA. 

Dave Maass
EFF

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2281470/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/family information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Section 7(1)(f) exempts from release “Preliminary drafts, notes, recommendations, memoranda and other records in which opinions are expressed, or policies or actions are formulated, except that a specific record or relevant portion of a record shall not be exempt when the record is publicly cited and identified by the head of the public body.” [5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(f)]. Section 7(1)(n) exempts records relating to a public body's adjudication of employee disciplinary cases. Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). Materials subject to attorney-client privilege and work-product privilege have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m) which exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body. Communications between a public body and an attorney or auditor representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body, and materials prepared or compiled with respect to internal audits of public bodies. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: ShotSpotter and SoundThinking
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with SoundThinking or ShotSpotter from November 1, 2023 to present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Stefana Breitwieser
